articles.title: Beyond Lovable and Mistral: 21 European startups to watch
articles.description: It is not that European startups never get attention ÔÇö Lovable and Mistral AI are proof of that. But there are many more that insiders are tracking.

articles.title: Uber wants to turn its millions of drivers into a sensor grid for self-driving companies
articles.description: Praveen Neppalli Naga, Uber's chief technology officer, revealed the plan in an interview at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, describing it as a natural extension of a nascent program the company announced in late January called AV Labs.

articles.title: ReplitÔÇÖs Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why heÔÇÖd rather not sell
articles.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now: in a world where rival Cursor is reportedly in talks to be acquired by SpaceX for $60 billion, is Replit also bound to sell?

articles.title: Musely secures $360M from General Catalyst without giving up equity
articles.description: The DTC skin, hair, and menopause care brand will use the non-dilutive capital to super-charge customer acquisition.

articles.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
articles.description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

articles.title: Coatue has a plan to buy up land for data centers, possibly for Anthropic
articles.description: Coatue, one of the biggest names in venture capital, has a new venture that is reportedly buying land near large power sources.

articles.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
articles.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

articles.title: Ubuntu services hit by outages after DDoS attack
articles.description: A group of hacktivists have claimed responsibility for a distributed denial-of-service attack, which has affected several Ubuntu and Canonical websites, and prevented users from updating the Linux-based operating system.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
articles.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in court, and there are plenty more witnesses to come. Musk’s argument against OpenAI? By converting the company to a for-profit model, Sam Altman betrayed the ÔÇťnonprofit for the […]

articles.title: People are finally using RedditÔÇÖs search
articles.description: The company saw a 30% year-on-year jump in the number of people using search every week, CEO Steve Huffman said on Thursday.

articles.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
articles.description: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals ÔÇö from avatars to cinematic portraits.

articles.title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales ÔÇö but a chip shortage looms
articles.description: Cook warned that Apple is facing supply-chain headwinds from RAMageddon that could impact its business.

articles.title: Y Combinator alum Skio sells for $105M cash, only raised $8M, founder says
articles.description: Subscription billing fintech Skio sold to its competitor Recharge in what was a healthy exit, according to its founder and former CEO.

articles.title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
articles.description: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI companyÔÇÖs latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
articles.description: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

articles.title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
articles.description: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf, and now have dueling ad campaigns.

articles.title: Rivian downsizes DOE loan to $4.5B for Georgia factory
articles.description: Rivian has reworked its loan deal with the Department of Energy and now expects to borrow $4.5 billion to build its new factory in Georgia, down from the original amount of $6.6 billion.

articles.title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel, used by millions of websites
articles.description: Web hosts are scrambling to fix the bug under active attack by hackers. One company said hackers have been abusing the bug for months.

articles.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
articles.description: OpenAI will begin rolling out its cybersecurity testing tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber only "to critical cyber defenders" at first.

articles.title: EV startup Faraday Future paid $7.5M to company tied to founder Jia Yueting
articles.description: The perpetually struggling EV company made the payments while being investigated by the SEC. That four-year probe was ultimately closed in March.

articles.title: Infrasound waves stop kitchen fires, but can they replace sprinklers?
articles.description: Acoustic fire suppression goes commercial.

articles.title: Study: AI models that consider user's feeling are more likely to make errors
articles.description: Overtuning can cause models to "prioritize user satisfaction over truthfulness.ÔÇŁ

articles.title: The RAMpocalypse has bought Microsoft valuable time in the fight against SteamOS
articles.description: Op-ed: Valve has made a dent in Windows' gaming share, but can it keep going?

articles.title: Man dies covered in necrotic lesions after amoebas eat him alive
articles.description: Doctors suspect three factors, each unremarkable on its own, contributed to his fate.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.description: The outage has hampered communication concerning a critical vulnerability that gives root.

articles.title: Senators ban themselves from prediction markets after candidates bet on own races
articles.description: Senator decries "blatant, brazen corruption," wants to target Trump admin next.

articles.title: Minnesota passes ban on fake AI nudes; app makers risk $500K fines
articles.description: More evidence of Grok CSAM seen as Minnesota passes nudifying app ban.

articles.title: Amazon stuck with months of repairs after drone strikes on data centers
articles.description: AWS stops billing Middle East cloud customers as repairs to war damage drag on.

articles.title: Scorpions go terminator mode and reinforce their weapons with metal
articles.description: Different hunting patterns seem to dictate different distributions of metal.

articles.title: GPT-5.5 matches heavily hyped Mythos Preview in new cybersecurity tests
articles.description: New results suggest Mythos' cyber threat isn't "a breakthrough specific to one model."

articles.title: Is your Purosangue SUV not sharp enough? Ferrari has you covered.
articles.description: We'll soon get to see the brand's first EV; first, a more honed V12 four-seater.

articles.title: Virgin Galactic reveals new ship, but it's running out of time and cash
articles.description: It's not clear whether Virgin Galactic has the cash reserves to fund a prolonged test phase.

articles.title: Apple may take "several months" to catch up to Mac mini and Studio demand
articles.description: Chip shortages and demand from AI enthusiasts are both playing a part.

articles.title: Women sue the men who used their Instagram feeds to create AI porn influencers
articles.description: AI ModelForge is a platform that teaches men how to generate their own AI influencers.

articles.title: Rocket Report: Falcon Heavy is back; Russia's Soyuz-5 finally debuts
articles.description: Two launches this week delivered 61 more satellites to orbit for the Amazon Leo broadband network.

articles.title: There's a lot of hype about Chinese EVsÔÇöis any of it true?
articles.description: In addition to being full of screens, China now wants its cars to be packed with AI.

articles.title: Trump nominates Fox News doctor to be the next surgeon general
articles.description: Trump lashes out at Cassidy while announcing his new nomination.

articles.title: US falls below Ukraine in press freedom as global autocracy takes hold
articles.description: "In 25 years, the average score... has never been so low."

articles.title: Russia cloaks launch schedule after spaceport falls in Ukraine's sights
articles.description: "We had serious inbound attempts to the cosmodrome that day."

articles.title: Elon Musk's 7 biggest stumbles on the stand at OpenAI trial
articles.description: Elon Musk spent three days testifying as the first witness in his trial against OpenAI.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck

articles.description: GoogleÔÇÖs sharing 20 fun facts to celebrate Google Translate turning 20, from its roots as a 2006 AI experiment to supporting almost 250 languages today.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.description: Google is bringing back its 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with Kaggle and registration is open.

articles.title: Introducing Advanced Account Security
articles.description: Introducing Advanced Account Security: phishing-resistant login, stronger recovery, and enhanced protections to safeguard sensitive data and prevent account takeover.

articles.title: Where the goblins came from
articles.description: How goblin outputs spread in AI models: timeline, root cause, and fixes behind personality-driven quirks in GPT-5 behavior.

articles.title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age
articles.description: OpenAI scales Stargate to build the compute infrastructure powering AGI, adding new data center capacity to meet growing AI demand.

articles.title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
articles.description: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age, focused on democratizing AI-powered cyber defense and protecting critical systems.

articles.title: Our commitment to community safety
articles.description: Learn how OpenAI protects community safety in ChatGPT through model safeguards, misuse detection, policy enforcement, and collaboration with safety experts.

articles.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
articles.description: OpenAI GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents are now available on AWS, enabling enterprises to build secure AI in their AWS environments.

articles.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
articles.description: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.

articles.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
articles.description: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended agreement that simplifies the partnership, adds long-term clarity, and supports continued AI innovation at scale.

articles.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
articles.description: Learn how Symphony, an open-source spec for Codex orchestration, turns issue trackers into always-on agent systemsÔÇöboosting engineering output and reducing context switching.

articles.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
articles.description: How Choco used OpenAI APIs to streamline food distribution, boost productivity, and unlock growthÔÇöan in-depth customer story on real-world AI impact.

articles.title: Our principles
articles.description: Our mission is to ensure that AGI benefits all of humanity. Sam Altman shares five principles that guide our work.
